properties.remark: TREE ON POWER LINE. POWER OUT TO 33 CUSTOMERS.
HIGHWAY 281 CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS DUE TO HIGH WINDS AND A DUST STORM.
LARGE TREE BLOWN DOWN ONTO ADAMS COUNTY COURTHOUSE.
VISIBILITY LESS THAN A QUARTER MILE AT TIMES DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
VISIBILITY NEAR ZERO ON WHEELER ROAD.
STATE ROUTE 17 CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS FROM MP 34 TO MP 50 DUE TO HIGH WINDS AND A DUST STORM.
SHINGLES BLOWN OFF A ROOF. VISIBILITY REDUCED TO LESS THAN 5 MILES DUE TO BLOWING DUST.

STORM SURVEY AUTHENTICATES EF0 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF MOSES LAKE ON NE ROAD P. NON-CONTINUOUS TRACK FROM SOUTH TO NORTHWEST...FOR 1/2 TO 1/3 OF A MILE. ESTIMATED WIDTH 300 FEET. ESTIMATED WINDS 65 TO 85 MPH. LIFTED ROOF OF OLD BARN 20 FEET OFF THE GROUND AND DROPPED IT 30 FEET FROM ITS ORIGINAL LOCATION ONTO ANOTHER BARN. DAMAGED GRAIN SILO. NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES DOWN...INCLUDING ONE TOSSED 300 FT FROM THE TREE. NUMEROUS SHINGLES SPREAD 120 YARDS. TIME ESTIMATED 615-620 PM PDT.

TORNADO TOUCHDOWN REPORTED 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF MOSES LAKE ON NE ROAD P. TORNADO ESTIMATED 300 YARDS WIDE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1/2 TO 3/4 OF A MILE. LIFTED AN OLD BARN 20 FEET OFF THE GROUND AND DROPPED IT 30 FEET FROM ITS ORIGINAL LOCATION. TIME ESTIMATED 615-620PM PDT.

*** 1 INJ *** ROOF RIPPED OFF THE LA QUINTA INN NEAR THE WA/ID STATE LINE WEST OF MOSCOW. SCREENS RIPPED OFF WINDOWS. A CONCRETE PLANTER HIT A FEMALE RESULTING IN AN INJURY. FUNNEL CLOUD SPOTTED WEST OF HOTEL BEFORE STORM HIT. REPORT FROM MSO SPOTTER.

FOUR TO FIVE PINE TREES DOWNED BY DOWNBURST WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM. YARD FURNITURE WAS TOSSED ABOUT AND TWO SMALL EMPTY TRAILERS WERE OVERTURNED. TWO CAMPERS HAD MINOR DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS SMALL TREE LIMBS DOWNED.
WATER RUNNING DOWN THE ROAD AND DITCHES ALONG HIGHWAY 2
DEPTH OF HAIL ON THE GROUND IS 6 INCHES

SEVERAL FARMERS IN THIS AREA REPORTED WIND GUSTS TO APPROXIMATELY 60 MPH AS WELL AS FIELD EROSION FROM BRIEF HEAVY RAINS. THERE WERE ALSO REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO THE WHEAT AND BARLEY FIELDS FROM THE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN.
TWO CAR ACCIDENT ON HIGHWAY 261 SOUTH OF RITZVILLE. HIGHWAY HAS BEEN CLOSED.
BLOWING DUST AND ZERO VISIBILITY HAS CLOSED COUNTY ROAD 20-NE.
COUNTY ROAD 18-NE CLOSED NEAR STRATFORD DUE TO BLOWING DUST AND ZERO VISIBILITY.
TREES DOWN...PEA-SIZED HAIL...HEAVY RAINFALL
LARGE TREES DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED AT 340 PM.
ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUST...FLOODING WITH SIX INCHES OF STANDING WATER...AND PEA-SIZED HAIL
LINCLON COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED ROAD WASHOUTS ALONG HIGHWAY 23 BETWEEN HARRINGTON AND SPRAGUE.
CHELAN COUNTY SHERIFF OFFICE REPORTED ROAD WASHOUTS ALONG NUMBER 1 AND NUMBER 2 CANYON ROADS. MANHOLE COVERS WERE BLOWN OFF IN DOWNTOWN WENATCHEE WITH STANDING WATER ON SOME ROADS.

MUDSLIDE ON SQUILLCHUCK ROAD
FLOODING ON LINVILLE DRIVE THREATENING HOUSES.

MUDSLIDE CLOSED HIGHWAY 97A BETWEEN MILEPOSTS 220 AND 222. ROAD REOPENED TO 1 LANE OF TRAFFIC BY 8AM ON THE 29TH.
TREES BLOWN OVER.
HOLES IN FIBERGLASS PORCH ROOFING
DEBRIS FLOW WITH ROCKS REPORTED OVER US ROUTE 97A NORTH OF SUNNYSLOPE. SMALL STREAM FLOODING HAS BEEN REPORTED ALONG THE HIGHWAY AS WELL IN THE SAME AREA.
TWO ROCK AND MUD SLIDES CLOSING HIGHWAY 97-A SOUTH OF ENTIAT. MILE MARKER 210-4 MILES SOUTH ENTIAT AND MILE MARKER 206-8 MILES SOUTH OF ENTIAT.

STATE ROUTE 261 FROM RITZVILLE TO NORTH OF WASHTUCNA... MILEPOST 40 TO 62...CLOSED DUE TO LOW VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST AND STRONG WINDS.
WENATCHEE WORLD REPORTED THE MCLAUGHLIN CANYON RD...5 MILES SOUTH OF TONASKET WASHED OUT AT APPROXIMATELY 7PM SUNDAY EVENING DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO NEAR LAWYER CANYON AND NEW HOPE ROAD BETWEEN NEZ PERCE AND CRAIGMONT.
CORRECTED FOR LOCATION....LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT RELAYED MESSAGE OF A TORNADO WITNESSED BY THE PUBLIC JUST SOUTH OF NEW HOPE ROAD NEAR LAWYER CANYON APPROX 6 MILES SE OF CRAIGMONT.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT RELAYED MESSAGE OF A TORNADO WITNESSED BY THE PUBLIC JUST SOUTH OF NEW HOPE ROAD NEAR LAWYER CANYON APPROX 6.4 MILES SW OF NEZ PERCE.
SMALL BRANCHES 2 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BROKEN OFF. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH.
